title: Data access control bypass via client-supplied query permission context on card creation
description: An authenticated, low-privilege user could read data from databases or tables they were not permitted to access because query handling trusted client-supplied permission context. On card creation the POST /api/card endpoint called card/check-run-permissions-for-query on the raw client-supplied query, which still carried a client-controlled :query-permissions/perms context; that context was honored during the permission check (and execution) instead of being re-derived server-side from the requesting user's group memberships and sandboxing, letting the caller execute native SQL against tables or databases outside their granted scope. The card update path (check-allowed-to-modify-query) had the same flaw.
